description_long: This object is an irregularly shaped stone with a rugged, angular profile and a mix of rough and smoother surfaces. The stone displays a combination of muted blue-gray and reddish-brown hues, with some lighter streaks and patches that suggest mineral deposits or weathering. Its surface is highly textured, with deep grooves, ridges, and protrusions, giving it a natural, unrefined appearance. One side features a prominent reddish-brown face, while the opposite side is dominated by blue-gray tones and intersecting linear features that may be veins or fractures. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings. Its overall form suggests it could serve as a natural barrier or obstacle in an urban park, roadside, or landscaped area.
description: A rugged, irregular stone with blue-gray and reddish-brown surfaces, featuring deep grooves and a highly textured, natural appearance.
description_view_0: This view highlights the angular, blue-gray upper surface and a vertical reddish-brown face, showing the stone's layered and jagged texture.
description_view_1: From above, intersecting linear features and grooves are visible, with a dominant blue-gray coloration and lighter streaks.
description_view_2: This side view emphasizes the stone's height and vertical ridges, with a mix of blue-gray and lighter mineral streaks.
description_view_3: The reddish-brown face is most prominent here, contrasting with the surrounding blue-gray surfaces and rough, uneven texture.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or natural barrier
attribute_pattern: Irregular veining and mottling with some linear grooves and mineral streaks; no artificial carvings.
attribute_weathering: Rough and eroded with visible grooves and color variation, indicating prolonged exposure to natural elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured or paved surfaces, but may blend with other natural stones or vegetation in landscaped settings.
